page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8163 cores running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the ecs.c5.large in the lower performance tiers. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench is weak, falling into the bottom 25% of tested servers, while multi-core performance drops into the bottom 10% with a stress-ng score of 1461.5 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 1114.0. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with uncached read and write speeds for large blocks measured at 10.04 GB/sec and 9.48 GB/sec respectively. Database and web serving operations reflect these limitations, yielding 1.51 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 1.29 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers a dedicated CPU allocation at a low entry cost, but exhibits low performance-per-resource efficiency due to its bottom-tier benchmark results. The lack of bundled local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage. Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU cores, this instance is best suited for lightweight compute tasks, basic web serving, small-scale utility scripting, or development and testing environments that require dedicated CPU execution without high memory or I/O demands.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 4.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate weak to poor performance, with single-core CPU capabilities in the bottom 25% and multi-core performance in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is also highly constrained, with uncached read speeds of 10.04 GB/sec. Given its dedicated CPU allocation but low overall performance-per-resource profile, the ecs.c5.large is best suited for lightweight compute workloads, basic web serving, and development or testing environments that do not require high memory or storage I/O.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.large is a compute-optimized instance with 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering entry-level performance for lightweight workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly, compute-optimized instance with dedicated virtual cores for lightweight processing and basic development environments.

4.0 GB of system memory with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
No bundled local storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
KVM hypervisor virtualization with dedicated CPU allocation
Suited for lightweight compute tasks, basic web serving, and testing
